entry.title: Re: can we maintain same TM purchase organization to diff...
entry.content:
Hello Krish,

In SAP TM there are couple of ways to determine que Organization structure.

If you build your Org. Structure in  TM under Tx. PPOME with the Company Code as head and Purchase Organization and Purchase Group under the Company code the only way to determine the Company code in the FO/FB is activate the  Subcontract. Checks ( This check if the ERP Company Code in FU matching with the TM company Code for planning, if not equal you're not able to planning.)in the Customizing in the Document type, in this way the Company Code will determine in FO/FB but, under the Organizational Structure you're able to have one Purchase Organization under the Company Code.

Refer to : 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/7577baedc3e045538e4c7bd49a234d3e.html?q=Organizational+Structure

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/d8370842983048b4bc16182d4dbee5a1.html?q=PPOME

in the last version of S/4 now a new condition is available to determine all the Organizational Structure check the blog below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-tm-new-way-to-determine-the-organizational-data-procuring-company-code/ba-p/13917341

Regards
Rogerio

entry.title: Re: Data Action: Copying user-submitted data to another v...
entry.content:
Not if data locking has been correctly configured in the model with Cost Centre as a driving dimension. With this configuration in place, you are able to lock just a specific cost centre if you want.

The problem I see with data locking however, is that data locks can only be set by a user who is identified as a Data Locking Owner in the model (in the version dimension). If you are a Data Locking Owner, then you can set the locks via script or otherwise, but the locks don't actually apply to you and a Data Locking Owner for a data slice can still make changes to the data while it is in a restricted state.

There is a chance that you could use calendar tasks to set the locks, so that your users are not Data Locking Owners, but this would depend on your business process (it doesn't work for us, as we have tried this). 

If you have Data Access Control enabled for the Cost Centre dimension, is it necesary for the data action to copy data to a different version? This would prevent other users changing data they should not be able to.

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Re: The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
entry.content: Referring to @Manan_Bansal issue, the beginning of this blog says about the overview of Joule's capabilities and says nothing about Joule would help in creation of RAP models or BO. In this context I find it interesting that Manan found a way Joule could do at least something for the BO. To @lienards does Joule have now the capability (or will be in future be capable) of creating a full blown RAP/CAP application for custom Z-tables and even create at least a skeleton to add own behaviours/determinations/actions? And would it do that either based on my prompts (where I'd give my Z-table definitions and what kind of behaviour I would like have on my app) or would it be based on my pre-made Z-table definions and CDS-views and I just prompt Joule to make an app for me.  For the moment I haven't done anything with Joule, just read blogs, documentation and SAP's youtube videos. But I am having side-by-side scenario request, where there would 2 Z-tables on BTP ABAP (header and item) and a need to maintain them on 1 LUW with capability to press a button to create a standard SAP sales order using SO API and thought Joule would help that from scratch upto the point where I'd implement the behaviour (eg the API call).

entry.title: Re: Data Action: Copying user-submitted data to another v...
entry.content: Data locking usually is a planning process related task not handled directly with Data action. Currently you have option to automate a data locking task in calendar at a certain point of planning process. And you can data lock by each costcenter if you use costcenter as data locking driving dimension.

entry.title: Re: Data Action: Copying user-submitted data to another v...
entry.content: @Ivan_C But from what I have seen, in a multiaction that can trigger data locking, you can only enable it over the whole dimension. It would literally not change anything. if i use restricted access for the cost center responsibles, then all of them will still be able to edit their cost centers. I think you misunderstood what I intend to do. All planners work in one story and trigger the same data action, they all work at different times. When one planner "submits" his planning, by triggering the data action, data locking should relate only to his cost center. When the next planner submits, then his cost center as well should be added to the lock. I dont want to enable data locking for all cost centers at the same time.

entry.title: Welcome Back — Preparing for a Record-Breaking 2025 Holiday Season
entry.content:
E-commerce continues its rapid evolution, with the 2024 holiday season setting new benchmarks. SAP Commerce Cloud played a pivotal role, processing $12.2 billion in Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) during Cyber Week—a 49.5% year-over-year increase—while maintaining 100% uptime . Notably, Cyber Monday experienced a 71.8% surge in GMV compared to the previous year, highlighting shifting consumer behaviors.

Globally, online holiday sales reached $1.2 trillion, with mobile devices accounting for a significant portion of purchases.

To support our customers in navigating this dynamic landscape, SAP is launching the 2025 Holiday Season Readiness Program (HSRP). This initiative offers strategic guidance and resources to ensure seamless operations during peak shopping periods.

Key Events Covered in the 2025 HSRP:

  • Singles’ Day – Tuesday, November 11, 2025
  • El Buen Fin – Friday, November 14 to Monday, November 17, 2025
  • Black Friday / Cyber Monday – Friday, November 28 to Monday, December 1, 2025
  • Christmas & Holiday Period – Wednesday, December 24 to Friday, December 26, 2025

While these events have evolved to the peak periods, we're aware that many of our customers do promotions outside these dates. In this case please refer to the and consult your Customer Success Manager for tailored support or directly reach out to SAP-HSRP@sap.com.

With proactive planning and collaboration, let's make the 2025 holiday season our most successful yet.

Stay tuned! The registration link will be published HERE!

For first-time SAP users navigating their inaugural holiday season, this blog post offers essential information and an introduction to what you can expect from SAP and what we require from you. For our long-term customers and partners using SAP Commerce Cloud to drive sales events during the holiday season, this post is aimed to serve as an insightful resource.

Who Can Participate?

The 2025 HSRP is open to all SAP Commerce Cloud customers planning promotional activities during key holiday events, including Singles’ Day, El Buen Fin, Black Friday/Cyber Monday, and the Christmas period. While traditionally focused on B2C businesses, B2B and B2B2C customers anticipating increased traffic during these times are also encouraged to participate.

What Support Does the Program Offer?

Participants in the 2025 HSRP will receive:

  • AI driven and in-depth Database and Platform Analysis and Recommendation: Collaborative assessments to determine if temporary resource scaling is necessary to handle anticipated traffic surges. With your input and your historical data, we can anticipate what you will need.

  • Preparation Checklists: Detailed guides outlining essential preparation steps for both customers and partners.

  • Tailored Recommendations: Access to SAP resources and articles to support optimal system performance.

Participation is free and adds an extra layer of assurance during peak periods. While customers can manage sales events independently, enrollment in the HSRP is recommended for an enhanced support level.

Why Is Customer Enrollment Important?

At SAP we can't foresee what our customers' plans are, how much load they expect on which system, how many promotions they will run. Collaborative planning is crucial during high-traffic periods. By enrolling in the HSRP, customers provide SAP with insights into expected traffic patterns, enabling proactive support and resource allocation to maintain optimal system performance.

2025 HSRP Timeline

  • Registration Period: June 24 – August 29, 2025.

  • Post-Registration: As soon as our customers enroll, SAP will start with the preperations, However, latest in September SAP will reach out with timelines, guidance, best practices, and additional resources to ensure readiness for the holiday season.

FAQs

What if customers are uncertain about expected holiday traffic?

Enrollment is still encouraged. SAP will use several tools to help forecast traffic and include appropriate buffers.

What if a customer is still in the project phase but plans to go live before the holiday season?

The HSRP is designed for both live clients and those planning to go live before the events. If a customer registers but doesn't go live before the holiday season, it's not an issue for SAP.

What if a customer misses the registration deadline or is unsure about running sales events?

Customers can still conduct sales events. SAP has standard procedures for surge events, requiring notification at least three business days in advance via a support ticket to ensure adequate resource provisioning. However, for the Holiday Season it's still better if SAP is informed as early as possible.

What if a customer plans promotions outside the official HSRP major traffic dates?

The SAP Commerce Cloud preventive business down management is active 24/7. However, due to the mass of critical events, customers are advised to highlight this during the registration or separately via their Customer Success Manager. Customers should notify SAP at least three business days in advance (SAP Commerce Cloud Services Description; 5.5 Scalability) through a support ticket to ensure proper support.

How will customers be informed about changes to their environments?

SAP will raise customer-facing tickets for scaling activities with a 3 weeks notification period, using the provided SOC S-user and/or provided contact. The timeline to inform customers about infrastructure changes will be communicated after the registration period. In previous years, the changes happened in October and November.

How can customers ask questions or provide feedback?

Feedback can be directed to Customer Success Partners (CSPs) or sent directly to the HSRP management via the SAP-HSRP inbox (SAP-HSRP@sap.com).

How can customers subscribe to receive notifications for their SAP Commerce Cloud solution?

Customers should subscribe to the SAP Cloud System Notification Subscription (CSNS) service to receive important system notifications. More information is available on the SAP Support Portal.

For more information on the 2025 HSRP and to register, please reach out.

Disclaimer

There is no legal or contractual basis for SAP to deliver this Holiday Season Readiness Program. It is a voluntary initiative by SAP to achieve the best possible result together with its customers.

If customers wish a level of support for the holiday season preparation that exceeds the standard preparations of this program, they can get in touch with the SAP Commerce Cloud Services Team. The consultants can offer an all-around carefree package that is more tailored to the individual needs of individual clients.

entry.title: Updating UTR Number in Payment Reference Field After Bank Reconciliation and in Payment Advice
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Please help us in getting a solution for the below related to Bank Reconciliation and Payment Advice.

Requirement:
• Customer requires a Payment advice to be generated and sent to the Vendor which contains
details of the Bank payment and the details of the various supplier invoices which were
cleared under that payment.
• Payment advice available and generated after running the Automatic Payment Program (APP)
does not carry the Payment reference provided by the Bank after the fund transfer.
• The requirement is to update the Payment document line item with the Payment reference
provided by the Bank, as a subsequent step to posting the document under APP, to enable
the reference to be included in the Payment advice.
Queries:
• Through the multi-bank connectivity, the bank will send the payment reference in return for
each payment instruction sent from APP. What is the standard API to be used to update the
Payment reference in the already posted Payment document line item?
• Is there an output available in the standard correspondence to generate the Payment advice
which can include the Payment reference after the update.

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: New Product Compliance in SAP S/4Hana
entry.content:
What’s NEW? in Product Compliance -

  • Integrated Content from 3E (Embedded)
    • 200+ Compliance requirements for marketability
    • 10+ Compliance requirement for Dangerous Goods
    • 100+ Compliance requirement for SDS Authoring
    • 30K+ Embedded listed substance
    • 17K+ Embedded regulatory phrases
    • 800+ Embedded regulatory graphics

 

  • Classical Vs NEW

 

Capability Area

Classic Approach

New Approach

Compliance Data Foundation

Uses a standard structure (property tree) with basic phrase mapping provided by partners.

Built on a modern, consistent data model with ready-to-use phrases and field links — all included as standard.

Product Marketability & Chemical Compliance

Partner-provided content includes regulatory lists and rules covering over 330,000 substances, along with 150+ compliance checks. Focuses mainly on industrial chemicals.

Includes similar substance data and compliance rules out of the box. Future add-ons (planned by 3E) will extend support to other industries.

Dangerous Goods Management

Partner content helps classify substances based on UN standards and regulatory rules. Includes 15 built-in compliance rules with visuals and logic.

More intelligent: a machine learning-based service is in the works (by 3E) to help automate classification tasks.

Safety Data Sheet (SDS) & Label Management

Relies on partner templates and rules for SDS and labelling. Offers 400+ compliance checks focused on industrial use cases.

Adds smarter features: an “Intelligent SDS package” is being planned, with automated rule logic and label templates to make the process smoother and faster.

 

What’s Happening to EHS Expert?

The traditional EHS Expert tool is being phased out as its logic is built directly into the new system — but don’t worry, its capabilities aren’t going away. They’re just being handled differently now:

Functionality

How It's Handled Now in S/4HANA

Data Checks

The system automatically checks data consistency through the data management apps. No need for a separate expert tool.

Secondary Data Calculation

This logic is now embedded in compliance requirements. For example:
- Marketability checks use built-in “substance list check” rules.
- SDS content can be filled automatically using 3E’s Intelligent SDS authoring.

Marketability & DG Transport Checks

These checks are now a part of either:
- The compliance logic itself, or
- The integrated business process (like sales or logistics).

Execution of Compliance Logic

Logic is handled in two ways:
1. Directly inside the S/4HANA system using ABAP code.
2. Via external services, where the logic runs remotely and sends the results back into your S/4 system.

Custom Compliance Rules

You can still create your own compliance checks, based on standard logic templates already built into the system.

🧠 Bottom Line:

Instead of using a separate tool like EHS Expert, everything is now integrated, smarter, and more flexible, right inside S/4HANA — or through connected services when needed

 

What’s Happening to Global Label Management (GLM)?

SAP is modernizing how labels are managed and printed. Instead of using the traditional GLM tool, here’s how things are handled now — split between SAP S/4HANA and partner solutions:

Functionality

What Changes in S/4HANA

Label Data Handling

All label-related data and logic are now managed directly inside S/4HANA.
- Labels are treated as compliance requirements.
- The system collects and uses label data as part of this compliance setup.

Label Logic & Triggers

The logic to determine when and which label to use is built into:
- The compliance rules, or
- The integrated logistics processes (e.g. shipping, output).

Integration is triggered using SAP’s Output Management.

Label Printing & Design

Printing is done through partner solutions (not by SAP directly):
- SAP is working with partners like Loftware and Eurosoft-Plus.
- An open interface is being developed so customers can use the label system of their choice.
- For simple labels, S/4HANA may offer enough built-in functionality — no external tool needed.

 

SAP WWI is no longer utilized for label printing and for more Detail refer SAP NOTE: 3015382

 Transition from ECC – Classical to NEW Product Compliance

 

🧩 Breaking Down the Core Components

  1. Product Marketability & Component Compliance

Best practices /Scope Items are referred from below link https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023?region=DE

 

Cloud Scope IDBusiness ConditionModule
31H N/AFoundation
31G31H - Assess Marketability of a ProductProduct Marketability and Chemical Compliance
31JBD 9: The scope item of this document is an extension of the BD9 scope item Therefore, the BD9 must be implemented and tested beforehand.Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance
50JBNZ - Create New Open MM Posting Period                                                        J45 - Procurement of Direct MaterialsProduct Marketability and Chemical Compliance
40LBNZ - Create New Open MM Posting Period                                                     J45 - Procurement of Direct Materials                                    BJ8 - Make-to-Stock - Process Manufacturing Based on Process OrderProduct Marketability and Chemical Compliance

 

When a company develops or sells a product, it’s essential to make sure that product complies with all relevant laws, industry regulations, and internal standards. That’s where Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance comes in—it helps organizations manage chemical compliance effectively across all products.

This tool plays a key role in making sure products are eligible for sale, shipment, and distribution by supporting regulatory compliance throughout the entire product lifecycle. It’s also integrated with sales and delivery processes to catch any issues early, helping prevent products from being shipped to regions where they may be restricted or not yet approved.

Ensures that products and raw materials meet regulatory and stakeholder requirements across all target markets.

Key features include:

  • Automated compliance checks for new products
  • Integrated marketability assessments
  • Substance volume tracking
  • Centralized monitoring of compliance status

With this in place, companies can avoid compliance-related shipment blocks or legal violations before they happen.

 

Product Marketability – Substance volume tracking

Data Management & Configuration Tasks

Here’s what you can configure and manage directly in the system:

  • Define your legal entities and map them for compliance tracking
  • Set up and manage bonded warehouses (for tracking-exempt storage)
  • Mark certain plants or companies as excluded from tracking
  • Choose which document types or item categories should be skipped
  • Select movement types that are relevant for tracking compliance
  • Assign import responsibilities for supplier-provided materials

This flexibility ensures that only the right transactions and materials are monitored.

How Substance Volume Tracking Works??

SAP offers multiple tracking modes to help you stay compliant with regulated substance limits:

  • Initial Tracking: For first-time substance flow recording
  • Periodic Tracking: To regularly monitor volumes over time
  • Manual or Automated Re-tracking: When changes or corrections are needed
  • Flexible Monitoring: You can define your own tracking cycles and get alerts based on volume thresholds

Regulatory Coverage (Current Scope)

The system supports several major global regulations, including:

  • EU REACH, UK REACH, and Turkey’s KKDIK
  • US TSCA Inventory, SNUR, and Chemical Data Reporting (CDR)

 

  1. Compliance Document Management

 Managing SDS and label documents is critical for both legal compliance and customer safety.

SAP streamlines this process with:

  • Centralized SDS management per product and country
  • AI-assisted document processing
  • Automatic SDS distribution post-shipment
  • Integration into logistics and delivery flows

Best practices /Scope Items are referred from below link https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023?region=DE

 

Cloud Scope IDBusiness ConditionModule
31H N/AFoundation
3VR1LQSafety Data Sheet and Label Management
3VQBD9 The scope item of this document is an extension of the BD9 scope item. Therefore, BD9 must be implemented and tested first.Safety Data Sheet and Label Management
6AVN/ASafety Data Sheet and Label Management
6MT1LQSafety Data Sheet and Label Management
7DO1LQSafety Data Sheet and Label Management

This reduces manual effort, improves accuracy, and ensures that customers get the right documents at the right time.

 

Managing Safety Data Sheets (SDS) in SAP S/4HANA

Managing Safety Data Sheets is essential for compliance and safe product handling. SAP S/4HANA makes it easy by embedding SDS processes directly into your operations.

Data Management

  • Reuse existing data from your product marketability setup
  • Apply the correct SDS rules per country to ensure you meet all local regulations

🛠️ Definition & Validation

You can define and manage SDS content smoothly with built-in tools:

  • Set up which SDS documents are required for different products and countries
  • Upload and officially release SDS documents into the system
  • Use predefined reports and analytics to track your SDS status
  • All this data serves as the foundation for integration with logistics processes

🔗Process Integration

SAP integrates SDS compliance checks throughout your business flows:

  • Automatically checks for SDS availability in key documents like:
    • Sales orders
    • Sales contracts
    • Outbound deliveries
    • Stock transport orders
  • If an SDS is missing, the system can:
    • Alert the logistics team
    • Trigger a request for the SDS from the responsible steward
  • Once ready, SDS documents can be:
    • Automatically sent to the customer when goods are shipped
    • Re-shipped later as needed, based on regulatory rules (e.g., US & EU)
    • Manually shipped when required
  • The system also supports side-by-side use with classic Product Compliance tools — so you can transition smoothly without disruption.

 

  1. Dangerous Goods Management

Any time a company produces, sells, or ships a product, it’s responsible for making sure that product complies with all applicable laws, industry rules, and internal guidelines.

Dangerous Goods Management helps companies handle this responsibility when it comes to hazardous materials. It allows you to assess both packaged and unpackaged goods, classify them based on relevant regulations, and determine whether they can be transported freely, under certain conditions, or not at all.

The system also supplies classification data for checks within key business documents, such as stock transfer orders, sales orders, and delivery notes. If a product involves dangerous goods, these checks automatically determine if transportation is permitted. For example, if a certain type of transport is banned in a particular region, the system will block the shipment. If transportation is only allowed under specific conditions, it will issue a warning. And if the product hasn’t yet been evaluated for dangerous goods compliance, the related documents will be put on hold.

Best practices /Scope Items are referred from below link https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023?region=DE

 

Cloud Scope IDBusiness ConditionModule
31H N/AFoundation
3FC31HDangerous Goods Management
3G83FC and BD9Dangerous Goods Management

 Transporting hazardous materials requires strict regulatory control. SAP ensures safe, legal handling through:

  • Classification of packaged and unpackaged dangerous goods
  • Integration with global regulations like IMDG, IATA, and ADR
  • Dangerous goods checks in sales, freight, and delivery
  • Automatic generation of compliant documents and labels

This guarantees safe logistics and prevents costly delays in shipping.

 

 

🤖Powering Compliance with Digital Intelligence

SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance uses embedded intelligence to:

  • Automate risk assessments and document generation
  • Stay updated with every regulatory change
  • Track compliance across the product lifecycle
  • Visualize impact using dashboards and analytics

With pre-built regulatory logic and substance databases (updated via the 3E content service), businesses gain trusted, current insights.

🌱Compliance Meets Sustainability

SAP also connects compliance with your ESG and sustainability goals through:

  • Carbon footprint tracking and accounting
  • Circular economy models and traceability
  • Extended producer responsibility (EPR) tools
  • Integrated emissions, safety, and waste metrics

This helps companies deliver on environmental commitments while reducing risks and waste.

🔄Seamless Integration Across the Supply Chain

SAP S/4HANA embeds compliance throughout the enterprise, enabling real-time checks and document generation across:

  • Product design – marketability and SDS planning
  • Procurement – supplier validations
  • Warehouse – compliance on goods receipt
  • Manufacturing – ingredient tracking and labelling
  • Sales & Delivery – SDS, dangerous goods, and blocking rules
  • Transportation – legal documents for logistics

This ensures auditability, traceability, and business continuity.

🚀Why It Matters

In today's global economy, compliance is not optional — it's foundational. Failing to meet even one requirement can:

  • Block product shipments
  • Trigger legal fines
  • Damage your brand reputation

With SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance, companies can:

  • Protect revenue
  • Innovate faster
  • Stay legally compliant
  • Advance sustainability goals
  • Build trust with customers and regulators

 

DATA MANAGEMENT BETWEEN NEW AND CLASSIC PRODUCT COMPLAINCE

3E HYBRID ADAPTOR -

  1. Go All-In on the New Product Compliance Solution
    • This is the ideal scenario for us long-term. However, due to current limitations with the Private Cloud setup, it's not realistically possible in 2023 or lower.
  2. Use the New System Just for Dangerous Goods
    • We could start by managing Dangerous Goods in the new system, while continuing to handle basic classification in the old (classic) setup.
    • This seems feasible and manageable in the short term.
  3. Fully Move Dangerous Goods to the New System
    • Shifting everything DG-related — including classification — to the new solution is also a viable option, if we have the needed support for packaging and regulatory filters.
  4. Use New Product Compliance Only for Product Marketability
    • Another path could be to leverage the new system just for Product Marketability, while keeping other functions in the classic environment.
  5. Combine DG and Marketability in the New System
    • We’re considering a hybrid option where both Dangerous Goods and Product Marketability are fully handled in the new solution.
    • This would include classification and packaging — but only if regulation-based packaging logic can be supported.
  6. Move Analytical Composition Data to the New System
    • We're also looking at migrating analytical composition data from the classic system.
    • That said, it’s still unclear whether this data can be completely transferred. Some logistics-related tasks might still need to run in the old system.

 

IMPORTANT SCENARIO –

When moving from the classic SAP system to the new Product Compliance setup, here’s what we can transfer and how it typically works:

  • Composition Data: Yes, we can move the composition information from the classic system to the new one.
  • SDS Report Generated: Can be transferred to New PC via Hybrid Adapter
  • SDS Raw Reports: We’re also able to transfer the raw Safety Data Sheet (SDS) reports from classic to new.
  • SDS Sections: (e.g. Physical & Chemical Properties): Specific sections like Section 3 on physical and chemical properties can also be carried over.
  • Real-World Practice: In most real scenarios, data in the classic system is maintained using ERC regulatory content, which supports smooth transitions and consistency during the migration.

✅Final Takeaway

SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance isn't just a system — it's a strategic enabler. With automation, analytics, and AI integration, companies can:

  • Shift from reactive to proactive compliance
  • Embed risk management across the product lifecycle
  • Ensure global readiness — in every market, every shipment

If you manufacture, sell, or ship products globally, this solution transforms compliance from a cost center to a competitive advantage.

@Michael_Schalk @Chr_Vogler @Saskia_Kalmbach @Michael_Mulder @Mohammed-Zahid @andrea_giessmann

entry.title: SAW 17: Question on the system load of file handling functions (xp_write_file, xp_read_file) ?
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP SQL AnyWhere 17.0.10 (8 cores), Windows Server 2016.

There's a large base:
- size - approximately 6 TB
- number of simultaneous connections - 300-500

The main size of the database falls on the texts of documents (word, excel, pdf, pictures), which are stored in the database itself (fields of long binary type), in separate tables located in separate tablespaces.

Users actively write to the database or read from the database, including quite active reading/writing of document texts. This creates quite a heavy load on the disk subsystem, which in turn leads to periodically noticeably lower responsiveness of the database server itself.

I have an option to change the way I store document texts, from storing them in the database to storing them in the file system (NTFS). In this case I will save them to the server using the system function xp_write_file, and read them using xp_read_file.

But this raises some questions: How will xp_write_file and xp_read_file behave under heavy load (when a couple hundred document files can be requested at the same time as several dozen other document files are being written) ?

Will transferring the storage of texts of documents from the database to the file system reduce the load on the disk subsystem, and if so, by how much ? and if yes, by how much ?

entry.title: Re: Data Action: Copying user-submitted data to another v...
entry.content:
Once you have configured the driving dimensions for data locking, a Data Locking step in a Multi Action will allow you to specify a scope. For example in the screenshot below, we have a model where we lock specific periods:

You can define parameters for these and then populate the parameter values via script for example if you are calling the multi action from a story or adding to a calendar task.

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Transform your Business with SAP Business AI' (Virtual, June 18, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual 

June 18, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Transform your Business with SAP Business AI – From innovation to impact’ taking place virtually on June 18th, 2025. Discover how SAP Business AI can deliver transformative value across business processes by breaking down functional silos and fueling cross-enterprise collaboration. Please find the current agenda here.

In this workshop, your will understand various aspects of leveraging SAP Business AI to drive innovation and achieve impactful business outcomes. Through engaging keynotes, customer success stories, and expert-led discussions, attendees will gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP’s AI strategy and its practical applications:

  • Learn about the transformative potential of SAP Business AI from industry leaders and discover how it can revolutionize enterprise data management and AI, empowering leaders to make more impactful decisions.
  • Hear firsthand experiences from customers using SAP Business AI across finance, supply chain, customer experience, and HR, highlighting how these solutions enhance efficiency and drive success.
  • Gain insights into SAP MaxAttention’s role in accelerating AI adoption, including best practices and key learnings from successful AI projects.
  • Learn about the evolution of Joule, the rise of Agentic AI, and the role of Knowledge Graphs in enriching AI agents with enterprise context for higher-quality outcomes. Experience the latest embedded AI innovations within the SAP Business Suite and understand their real-world business impact
  • Explore how AI-driven techniques facilitate custom code migration during SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) transformations. Understand how AI-enhanced tools such as SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and Tricentis optimize business processes, support application lifecycle management, and accelerate testing.

Your will walk away with a clear understanding of how SAP Business AI can transform their business operations, drive efficiency, and enhance decision-making. SAP MaxAttention will support your customers throughout their AI journey, from initial planning to full deployment, bridging the gap from AI Experimentation to AI Value realization.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, annchristin.schechter@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: How to Install GitHub Copilot for SAP ABAP IN Eclipse IDE via Genuitec?
entry.content:
How to Use GitHub Copilot for SAP ABAP in Eclipse

GitHub Copilot is a powerful AI assistant that can help streamline ABAP development in Eclipse by offering intelligent code suggestions and interactive chat support. This guide walks you through the setup and usage process tailored for SAP ABAP developers.

1. Prerequisites: Eclipse Version Compatibility

Ensure you are using Eclipse version 2025-03, as this is the minimum version required to support GitHub Copilot integration.

2. Installing GitHub Copilot Plugin via Genuitec

To integrate Copilot into Eclipse, follow these steps:

  1. Launch Eclipse.
  2. Navigate to Help > Install New Software.

 

  1. In the Work with field, enter the following URL:
       https://www.genuitec.com/updates/copilot4eclipse
  2. Wait for the available software list to load, then select Copilot4Eclipse.
  3. Proceed with the installation by clicking Next, accepting the license agreement, and completing the setup.

 

6. Restart Eclipse when prompted.

3. Access the Copilot Menu

After installation, a new Copilot menu will appear in the Eclipse toolbar. This menu provides access to all Copilot features, including login, preferences, and chat.

If you're wondering how this all works behind the scenes—Copilot4Eclipse (also known as Copilot for Eclipse) is a free plugin that brings GitHub Copilot’s AI magic right into your Eclipse IDE. It’s designed to feel native and professional, giving you a smooth, AI-assisted coding experience without the hassle.

The best part? It works with any GitHub Copilot subscription—whether you're on the Free, Pro, Business, or Enterprise plan. So no matter your setup, you can start coding smarter right away.

4. Sign In with GitHub

To activate Copilot:
Open the Copilot menu and select Sign In.

Authenticate using your GitHub credentials.

5. Subscription Options

Once signed in, you can choose between:

Free Trial:

  • 2,000 code completions per month
  • 50 chat messages per month

Paid Subscription:

  • Unlimited access to completions and chat
  • Access to advanced AI models (e.g., GPT-4)

6. Choosing the Right AI Model

After logging in, you can select the AI model that best fits your development needs:

  • Navigate to your GitHub Copilot settings.
  • Under the Features section, choose your preferred model.

Available Models:

  • GPT-4: More advanced and context-aware, ideal for complex ABAP logic, enterprise-grade development, and multi-layered class structures.

     

7. Using Copilot in ABAP Projects

Once configured, you can begin using Copilot in your ABAP development workflow:

  • Code Suggestions: As you type in the ABAP editor, Copilot will offer real-time, context-aware code completions.
  • Chat Assistance: Use the Copilot chat panel to ask questions like:
    • “How do I create a CDS view?”
    • “Suggest a method to calculate tax in ABAP.”

8. Customizing Preferences in Eclipse

Within the Copilot > Preferences menu, you can fine-tune how Copilot behaves in your environment.

Completion Model

Choose the AI model that Copilot should use for generating suggestions. This affects the quality and depth of the code completions.

Select Supported Languages

Specify which programming languages Copilot should support. For ABAP development, ensure ABAP is selected. You can also enable or disable support for other languages like Java, XML, or JavaScript depending on your project needs.

GitHub Copilot isn’t just a fancy autocomplete—it’s like having a smart coding buddy right inside Eclipse. For ABAP developers, it means less time stuck on syntax and more time building meaningful, high-quality SAP solutions. With helpful suggestions and an interactive chat at your fingertips, you can stay focused on what really matters.

That’s it! You’re all set to bring the power of GitHub Copilot into your ABAP development workflow. The setup is simple, the benefits are real, and the experience is actually pretty fun. So go ahead—install Copilot, start coding, and let the AI magic do its thing. Happy coding!

entry.title: HTTP Destination Request - "Missing dependencies" message
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Recently I'm not able to use HTTP Destination Request in SAP Build Apps. Last week it was working as expected, but this week whenever I try it, I get the "Missing dependencies. Please make sure to provide all dependencies in the input object." error message.

I tested it in two environments: Canary (EU12) and Live (EU10) - exactly the same issue in both environments.

An old application, already built and deployed works fine, but the same app (or any new) throws the same error in both preview and when deployed newly built.

Any idea what happens and how to solve it?

Jakub

entry.title: Re: Data Action: Copying user-submitted data to another v...
entry.content:
If you have a look through the documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/e07d46e950794d5a928a9b16d1394de6.html?locale=en-US then perhaps what you are after is 'Delegated Ownership of Data Locks'. According to the documentation, a delegated owner can set data locks to a more restrictive state but not the reverse. So this could cater to your requirement. You could certainly do this on a Cost Centre scope so a User could set the lock on just their own Cost Cenre scope without locking anyone else's. They also then couldn't unlock it again as a delegated owner.

Suggest you check this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/data-locking-ownership-in-sap-analytics-cloud-lessons-learned-and-the-need/ba-p/13927413

entry.title: Re: Archiving Procedure for Handling Units and Batches in...
entry.content:
Hi VincenzoN,

archiving in S/4HANA Public Cloud is done via ILM (Information Lifecycle Management).
Here it's the ILM object LE_HU for handling units.
You have to maintain ILM Policies and Rules.
Then you should be able to archive the handling units with the app "Archive Explorer".
Refer to this page: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/21f474bda2bf47f89008b2db3c00f055.html

Best regards,
Barış

entry.title: Percentage of Completion (POC) and Completed Contract Basis (CCB)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'd like to confirm my understanding of how revenue recognition works in SAP using Percentage of Completion (POC) and Completed Contract Basis (CCB) approaches. Can someone please tell me if the following flow is correct?

[POC: Percentage of Completion]

  1. MM Goods Receipt
    The finished product is received into inventory.
    Journal entry:
    Inventory / GR/IR

  2. SD Delivery
    When the product is delivered, it is issued from inventory and recorded as Cost of Sales.
    Journal entry:
    Cost of Sales / Inventory

  3. PS Result Analysis (Cost Transfer)
    The cost initially booked to Cost of Sales is transferred to WIP (Work in Progress) via PS.
    Journal entry:
    WIP / Cost of Sales

  4. PS Result Analysis (Progress-based Revenue Recognition)
    Revenue and receivables are recognized based on the progress percentage. This is an internal accounting entry, separate from SD billing.
    Journal entry:
    Accounts Receivable / Revenue

  5. SD Billing (Final Customer Invoice in the Closing Period)
    When SD billing is finally executed (typically in the final period), another entry for revenue and receivable is posted.
    Journal entry:
    Accounts Receivable / Revenue
    (Note: This revenue is separate from the one recognized in step 4.)

[CCB: Completed Contract Basis]

  1. MM Goods Receipt
    The finished product is received into inventory.
    Journal entry:
    Inventory / GR/IR

  2. SD Delivery
    Upon delivery, the product is issued from inventory and booked as Cost of Sales.
    Journal entry:
    Cost of Sales / Inventory

  3. PS Result Analysis (Cost Transfer)
    The cost is transferred to WIP using PS result analysis.
    Journal entry:
    WIP / Cost of Sales

  4. SD Billing (Upon Project Completion)
    In the completion period, billing is executed and revenue is recognized in full.
    Journal entry:
    Accounts Receivable / Revenue

Is this understanding correct for handling revenue recognition in SAP projects with SD, MM, and PS integration?
Any feedback or corrections would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - understand the new Archive Offerings
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

the transition from SAP ByD tenant to SAP ByD Archive tenant is mostly a formal process and not a technical one. The transition requires a new contract as specific terms have to be followed by the customer. This allows the customer to benefit from the different pricing but requires his commitment to follow the strict terms in usage of the specific tenant.

Best, Dirk

entry.title: Re: Display an error message in a Fiori application trigg...
entry.content:
Hello,

I had the same issue in MV45AFZZ userexit_save_document_prepare. What worked for me was first raising the GUI message and second using macros message_collect or message_collect_lord.
Your message needs to be in the SY-MSG* fields before you call the macros (whose the MESSAGE statement will fill).

* For SAPGUI VA02:
MESSAGE w001(00) WITH 'Test Message'.

* For Manage Sales Order app, both approaches below work:
* message_collect if_sd_sls_message=>co_bal_subobject-sd_sls-badicheckbeforesave if_sd_sls_message=>co_severity-warning.
message_collect_lord.

You can look at include MV45AF0C_CLOUD_EXTENSIBILITY at the end of form badi_check_before_save . 
Here you see how SAP handles the messages after the BADI and they always raise the message via the MESSAGE statement and via one of the two macros above. 

Best regards,
Stefan

entry.title: Why don't WorkZone pages print like normal web pages?
entry.content:
My users need to print Workzone pages to PDF from time to time - policies and guidance, that type of content.

The browser's print function doesn't work with Workzone the way other web pages do.

I can't show screen grabs for confidentiality, but workzone will only print the area of the page currently displayed in the viewport.:

  • If you're at the top of a long workzone page, the browser print function will print the top area of the page currently displayed
  • If you're scrolled part way down the page, print will only print the portion currently displayed

This is nuts.

I've never seen a browser print function behave this way before.

What's going on and how do I fix it?

entry.title: Re: Custom entity, $count, $skip, $top not supported
entry.content:
@patrick_weber11 

The following repository from @Andre_Fischer may help. Here, application logs are retrieved via custom entity and associated with the root entity. Note that an association is used instead of composition.

https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-application-jobs

define root view entity ZAPPR_InventoryTP_01
  as select from zapp_inven_01
   association [0..*] to ZAPPI_appl_log as _ApplicationLog     on $projection.LogHandle = _ApplicationLog.Log_handle

Here's the implementation of the query provider class: 

https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-application-jobs/blob/main/src/zapp_cl_show_appl_log.clas.abap

entry.title: Fixing Inconsistent Ratings Between Talent Review Form and Talent Card Mini Matrix
entry.content:
Recently, I encountered an issue where the performance and potential ratings displayed in the Talent Review form’s 9-box grid did not match the mini matrix grid shown on the Talent Card. This inconsistency could lead to confusion and affect succession planning decisions. In this blog, I’ll Walk you through the problem, the troubleshooting steps I took, and how I successfully resolved the issue to keep ratings synchronized across all views.

Issue Overview

In SAP SuccessFactors Talent Management, employees receive performance and potential ratings through a Talent Review form. These ratings generate a 9-box matrix that helps talent calibration and succession planning.

Succession planners use the Talent Card, which contains a mini version of the 9-box matrix, called the mini matrix inside the Performance & Potential section. This mini matrix should accurately reflect the ratings from the Talent Review form.

Issue Identified:
For one employee, the ratings in the Talent Review form and the mini matrix on their Talent Card were inconsistent, resulting in different 9-box placements. Since both are based on the same evaluation process, the ratings should be identical.

Troubleshooting Steps

I followed a step-by-step approach to diagnose and fix the problem:

Step 1: Matrix Grid Report Source Configuration

  • Action Taken:
    Navigated to the Matrix Grid Report – Performance-Potential and reviewed the data sources for the X-axis (Performance) and Y-axis (Potential).
  • Observation:
    The data source was set to “All Data Sources” which could pull inconsistent information.

  • Change Made:
    Updated the source to “Talent Review” to ensure the ratings were sourced directly from the Talent Review process.

Step 2: Validate Processes and Forms

  • Action Taken:
    Accessed Processes and Forms and opened the “Talent Review” process.
  • Observation:
    Only an older Talent Review form was selected; the current 2025 Talent Review form was missing.

  • Change Made:
    Selected the “Talent Review 2025 Template” and checked the “Include In-Progress Documents” box, allowing data from ongoing reviews to be included.

Step 3: Review People Profile Settings

  • Action Taken:
    Navigated to Configure People Profile and on General Setting.
  • Checklist:
    • Checked that the Talent Data Date Range did not match the active review cycle.

    • Therefore, I changed the dates to the active review cycle.
    • Ensured “Include Talent data from this process” is set to “Talent Review”, consistent with the process selected in Processes and Forms.
    • Ensured “Include In-Progress Documents” was enabled here as well.

Step 4: Talent Card Configuration

  • Action Taken:
    Opened Manage Talent Card, selected the Succession Talent Card, and accessed the Performance & Potential section.
  • Fix:
    Made sure the option “Use employee profile rating configuration” was enabled. This ensures that the mini matrix on the Talent Card pulls ratings consistent with the employee profile and Talent Review form.

Final Resolution

After implementing these changes:

  • The performance and potential ratings in the Talent Review form, Employee Profile, and Talent Card mini matrix became fully synchronized.
  • Succession planners now see consistent 9-box data across all tools, improving the accuracy and reliability of succession planning.

If you’re facing similar inconsistencies in SAP SuccessFactors Succession Management, these steps might help you ensure that ratings are aligned across all interfaces, supporting better talent decisions.
